mechanism_narrative: A lower 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the present value of future equity cash flows and supports risk appetite; when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.09 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if rolling 3-year walk-forward tests show 20-day returns after flat or falling FEDFUNDS are not above unconditional returns, or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses down by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while still above 5.5%, labor-market slack is healing without immediate wage-overheating pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses down by >0.3pp over 3 months while UNRATE > 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 6.7 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of cases or average triggered returns are not above baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in RSAFS and HOUST signals household demand broad enough to lift earnings expectations; when both series rise over 3 months, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1547.0 and RSAFS was 540915.0 in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-HOUST signal has lower 20-day forward returns than either single-series signal or delivers a 20-day hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and UNRATE is above 5.5%, policy remains easier than labor conditions require, suppressing discount rates while recovery continues; this produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.09 and UNRATE was 6.7 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if low-FEDFUNDS high-UNRATE states do not outperform other states over 20 trading days, or if the interaction term is negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly INDPRO decline greater than 1% signals renewed supply-side stress and weaker operating leverage; after this crossing, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 105.7244 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO drops below -1% month over month and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above a 3% annualized 3-month pace while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, inflation risk rises before policy normalizes, compressing equity multiples and producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 261.775 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09 in the latest available snapshot
regime_hint: early inflation-pressure phase under zero rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-at-zero-rate states produce nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the CPIAUCSL interaction with FEDFUNDS is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST year-over-year increase above 10% signals strong residential investment and durable-goods demand; after this threshold is crossed, S&P 500 returns are positive over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1547.0 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST year-over-year growth above 10% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth weakens over 3 months while RSAFS also falls, income and spending momentum deteriorate together, causing analysts to cut near-term earnings expectations and producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 142624.0 and RSAFS was 540915.0 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS and RSAFS 3-month declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.
